reasoning:
The Treasury is currently operating under extraordinary measures since the debt limit was reinstated in January 2025. Without the ability to issue net new debt, the Treasury General Account (TGA) will systematically decline to fund the deficit until legislation is passed. 
- By June 1, 2025: End-of-month and early-month outflows heavily drain the TGA. The balance will almost certainly fall below the May 25 baseline of 476B.
- By June 24, 2025: Expected June 15 tax inflows will provide a temporary boost, but are unlikely to restore the TGA to late-May levels given the lack of debt issuance and ongoing deficit spending. 
- By August 23, 2025: Large July and August spending typically drives the TGA to very low levels as the 'X-date' approaches, unless a debt ceiling deal has already passed. A deal is possible but not guaranteed by late August. 
- By November 21, 2025: It is extremely likely that the debt limit will have been resolved by fall, post which the Treasury will heavily issue bills to rapidly refill the TGA to its normal target (historically $700B+), easily surpassing the 476B mark. The probabilities strictly reflect rising uncertainty toward the longer horizons in accordance with prior instructions.
